blocks.body: Future Continuous: Forms and Use — will be + verb-ing for actions that will be in progress at a future moment.

{"level":"B1","intro":"In Future Continuous, we use will be + verb-ing to show an action that will be in progress at a certain time in the future.","rules":[{"label":"will be + verb-ing","color":"emerald","text":"The form is the same for all subjects: I / you / he / she / it / we / they will be working.","example":"I will be studying. / They will be waiting."},{"label":"Future point","color":"blue","text":"This tense highlights the process in the future, not only the fact or the decision.","example":"At 8 p.m. tomorrow, we will be having dinner."},{"label":"Background","color":"amber","text":"Future Continuous often shows an action as background for another future event.","example":"When you call, I will be driving home."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic formula","intro":"Start with the pattern that works with every subject.","items":[{"label":"All subjects","title":"will be + verb-ing","subtitle":"I will be working. / She will be waiting. / They will be sleeping."},{"label":"Short forms","title":"I'll be / he'll be / they'll be","subtitle":"We often use contractions in natural speech."},{"label":"Meaning","title":"action in progress in the future","subtitle":"The action will be happening at that time."},{"label":"Spelling","title":"write → writing / lie → lying / sit → sitting","subtitle":"Remember the usual spelling rules for the -ing form."}]}

{"title":"2. When we use Future Continuous","sections":[{"label":"Specific future moment","color":"emerald","description":"Show that an action will be in progress at a particular time in the future.","examples":[{"en":"This time tomorrow, I will be flying to Madrid.","ua":"At that time tomorrow, the action will already be happening."},{"en":"At 10 a.m. next Monday, she will be taking her exam.","ua":"The action will be in progress at that future moment."}]},{"label":"Longer future situation","color":"blue","description":"Use the tense for a process that will continue for some time in the future.","examples":[{"en":"All day tomorrow, we will be working on the project.","ua":"This highlights a longer future process."},{"en":"Later tonight, they will be driving back home.","ua":"The focus is on what will be happening during that period."}]},{"label":"Background for another event","color":"amber","description":"The tense works well as future background for another action or event.","examples":[{"en":"When you arrive, we will be eating dinner.","ua":"One action becomes the background for another."},{"en":"Next week at this time, I will be staying with friends.","ua":"We describe a situation that will already be in progress then."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick patterns","intro":"See how the form works in typical sentences.","rows":[{"en":"I will be studying at 8 p.m.","ua":"will be studying","note":"action in progress at an exact time"},{"en":"She will be waiting for us later tonight.","ua":"will be waiting","note":"future situation seen as a process"},{"en":"We will be travelling this time next week.","ua":"will be travelling","note":"looking at the future from a future point"},{"en":"They will be eating when you call.","ua":"will be eating","note":"future background for another event"}],"warning":"Future Continuous shows a process. If the decision, plan, or prediction matters more, will or be going to may sound more natural."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Leaving out be after will.","wrong":"❌ She will working at 9 p.m.","right":"✅ She will be working at 9 p.m."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using the base form instead of -ing.","wrong":"❌ We will be work late tomorrow.","right":"✅ We will be working late tomorrow."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Adding am / is / are after will.","wrong":"❌ They will are studying then.","right":"✅ They will be studying then."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["Future Continuous = will be + verb-ing for all subjects.","This tense shows an action that will be in progress in the future.","It works well for longer future situations and background for another event.","After will be, use the verb-ing form."]}
